basics.name: Joe Pegler
basics.label: Web3 Infrastructure Engineer · ERC-4337 Execution, Smart Accounts, and Cross-Chain SDKs
basics.summary: Web3 infrastructure engineer focused on ERC-4337 execution systems, smart accounts, and cross-chain SDK architecture across EVM and Solana. I design simulation-first execution pipelines with retries, fallback handling, sponsorship flows, typed SDKs, and structured operational errors that improve reliability in production.

selectedImpact: Designed an internal ERC-4337 transaction orchestration architecture with parallelized fee lookup, nonce handling, sponsorship, simulation, and execution paths to reduce latency versus provider-managed flows.
Hardened sponsored and gasless transaction execution with retries, simulation, fallback handling, ABI/Sourcify-backed error decoding, and structured operational errors.
Unified multi-provider quote and execution routing across LiFi, Jupiter, 1inch, Odos, Mayan, LayerZero, and Relay behind one typed execution surface.
Led SDK architecture for smart-account, quote, and transaction execution workflows used across frontend and backend teams.

Built @biconomy/abstractjs from scratch: architected the viem-inspired TypeScript SDK (modular clients, abstractions, entities), wrote the developer documentation, and shipped a package averaging ~5.2k weekly npm downloads for smart-account and cross-chain execution.
Built and open-sourced Statecraft, a TypeScript-first Ethereum integration testing framework for deterministic scenario-based tests with typed fixtures, forked state, and isolated runtimes.
Implemented EIP-712, EIP-1271, ERC-6492-style compatibility flows, and P-256/passkey-related signing support for smart-account UX.
whyHire: Turns unreliable blockchain execution paths into production-grade infrastructure.
Designs transaction systems with simulation, retries, fallback logic, typed errors, and operational debugging built in.
Bridges protocol complexity and product delivery through SDKs, execution services, and developer tooling.
bestFit: Early-stage teams needing senior ownership across architecture and implementation
availability: Open to full-time, long-term contract, or selective fractional work across protocol infrastructure, transaction execution, SDKs, reliability, and developer tooling.

work.name: defi.app
work.summary: Owned transaction infrastructure, sponsorship flows, execution reliability, and developer tooling for a unified cross-chain DeFi product.
highlights: Designed an internal ERC-4337 transaction orchestration system with parallelized fee lookup, nonce handling, sponsorship, simulation, and execution paths to reduce latency versus provider-managed flows.
Improved execution reliability with retries, fallback handling, EntryPoint v7 simulation, ABI/Sourcify-backed error decoding, and structured operational errors.
Unified multi-provider quote and execution routing across LiFi, Jupiter, 1inch, Odos, Mayan, LayerZero, and Relay behind one typed integration surface.
Standardized TypeScript SDK execution surfaces including RouteClient and ExecutionManager for frontend and backend teams.
Built EVM and Solana simulation and sponsorship infrastructure, including Jito-aware validation for sponsored transaction safety.

work.name: biconomy
work.summary: Led architecture and development of @biconomy/abstractjs, Biconomy's core TypeScript SDK for smart accounts and multi-chain transaction execution.
highlights: Built @biconomy/abstractjs from scratch: viem-inspired abstractions and entities, modular clients, batching, and account abstraction APIs; wrote the SDK documentation and examples.
Shipped a widely adopted developer package averaging ~5.2k weekly npm downloads, used by teams building gasless and account-abstracted product flows.
Implemented EIP-712, EIP-1271, ERC-6492-style account compatibility, and P-256/passkey-related signing flows for smart-account UX.
Hardened production readiness with typed errors, composable batching, Vitest, Bun, GitHub Actions, and Codecov.

work.name: enso
work.summary: Lead Frontend Engineer at Enso Finance. Shipped the primary Ethereum dApp from early product stages through production, with focus on client architecture, execution-related user flows, and integration surfaces for DeFi execution workflows.
highlights: Built and maintained frontend and client-side systems for Enso's primary Ethereum dApp—architecture, execution-related user flows, and production rollout—collaborating with backend and protocol engineers on integration boundaries, API contracts, and execution UX.
Contributed to Graph Protocol–based indexing used by the product for on-chain data, with clear integration boundaries to backend consumers and the client surface.
Contributed to intent/routing-adjacent product surfaces for decentralized trade execution, in collaboration with backend and protocol engineering.

work.name: coinflex
work.summary: Led trading interface architecture and release infrastructure for a live exchange product.
highlights: Led delivery of the trading interface, owning frontend architecture, core feature rollout, and engineering standards for a live exchange product.
Automated CI/CD for the interface stack, reducing release risk and improving deployment consistency.

earlierExperience: GameAnalytics - Lead Frontend Developer (2016 to 2019): led a team of five, defined frontend architecture and quality standards, and won company MVP 2018/19.
FSI, Healthcare 21, and Nixatel (2013 to 2016): built cross-platform enterprise applications and delivered 0 to 1 mobile and web products in small teams.
